title: LGE Section Family Contracts
summary: 9 section family contracts and global design guardrails.
markdown:
# LGE Section Family Contracts

## Global Principles

- Treat the page as a connected visual system, not isolated slot cards.
- Prefer strong first-pass hierarchy over repeated corrective loops.
- If a section changes, make the hierarchy, focal point, and rhythm visibly clearer than before.
- Use contrast, scale, spacing, and massing to create dominance before adding decorative detail.
- Preserve clone identity according to scope: broad page work may move more, but narrower component work must stay more faithful to the surrounding page identity.

## Families

### hero

- label: Hero Generation Contract
- targetSlots: hero
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Flat white slabs with weak contrast
- avoid: Headline and support cards competing at equal weight
- avoid: Tiny visual placeholders that collapse the stage

### quickmenu

- label: Quickmenu Generation Contract
- targetSlots: quickmenu, quickMenu, tabs
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Uniform cards with no dominant entry
- avoid: Inconsistent icon shells or image sizing
- avoid: Dense grids that compete with the hero stage

### ranking

- label: Ranking Contract
- targetSlots: best-ranking, ranking
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Flat list with no visual rank hierarchy
- avoid: Rank numbers that are too small to register
- avoid: Equal-weight item treatment that removes the ranking intent

### banner

- label: Banner Contract
- targetSlots: summary-banner-2, carebanner, labelbanner
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Undecided card with soft headline and no visual weight
- avoid: Equal-size headline and visual that split attention without a focal winner
- avoid: Text-only banner that uses the slot as a paragraph container

### commerce

- label: Commerce Grid Contract
- targetSlots: md-choice, timedeal, subscription, space-renewal, latest-product-news, brand-showroom, marketing-area
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Uniform cards with no lead/support distinction in promo contexts
- avoid: Dense thumbnail grids that eliminate product identity
- avoid: Mixed card styles within the same grid family

### benefit-hub

- label: Benefit Hub Contract
- targetSlots: benefit, benefit-highlight, mainService, bestcare, service-benefit
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Benefit blocks with long paragraph descriptions per item
- avoid: Icons at inconsistent scales that break grid rhythm
- avoid: Generic benefit language that could apply to any brand

### service-hero

- label: Service Hero Contract
- targetSlots: service-intro, hero
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Campaign glamour tone that replaces service clarity with visual drama
- avoid: Support cards that visually compete with the headline at equal scale
- avoid: Dark cinematic surfaces that conflict with the service-trust context

### service-tabs

- label: Service Tabs Contract
- targetSlots: quickMenu, tabs
- primaryGoal: 

- avoid: Tabs that visually compete with the intro headline above
- avoid: Tab labels that wrap to 2 lines in the default layout
- avoid: Tab styling that introduces a new color system disconnected from the page palette

### page-shell

- label: Page Shell Contract
- targetSlots: page-shell
- primaryGoal:
